chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يَذْبَحُ وَ يَحْتَشُّ لِدَابَّتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 98 – The one in Ihraam slaughters (an animal) and he cuts grass for his riding animal

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/329)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/329)
text: الْمُحْرِمُ يَذْبَحُ الْبَقَرَ وَ الْإِبِلَ وَ الْغَنَمَ وَ كُلَّ مَا لَمْ يَصُفَّ مِنَ الطَّيْرِ وَ مَا أُحِلَّ لِلْحَلَالِ أَنْ يَذْبَحَهُ فِي الْحَرَمِ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ فِي الْحِلِّ وَ الْحَرَمِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The one in Ihraam can slaughter the cow, and the camel, and the sheep, and whatever does not array its wings from the birds, and whatever is Permissible for the one not in Ihraam that he slaughters inside the Harram, so it is also Permissible for the one in Ihraam (to slaughter) in the unrestricted area as well as in the Harram (the restricted area)’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Hariz who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘In the state of Ihram, it is permissible for one to slaughter the cow, the camel and the sheep. One can slaughter all of such birds which do not flip their wings in flight; and whatever is lawful for one who is not in the state of Ihram to slaughter in al- Haram (the sacred area), is lawful for one in the state of Ihram in non-al-Haram (the sacred area) and in al-Haram (the sacred area).’”
